titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 66

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=135 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:02.920351+00 | translation moa=134 status=live modified=2016-06-07 15:00:51.783125+00

div.p:
Malcolmus filius Moregrundi Comitis de Mar . Omnibus Has Literas visuris uel audituris Salutem . Sciatis me Concessisse et Hac Carta mea
confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie sancti Andree apostoli de Scotia ⁊ Canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus ⁊ seruituris Ecclesiam sancti Muluoch de Taruelund quam pater
meus eis dedit cum decimis ⁊ oblationibus ⁊ omnibus Rectitudinibus eidem ecclesie pertinentibus . ⁊ terram que eidem ecclesie pertinet cum molendino in eadem
terra fundato . ⁊ unam carrucatam terre que dicitur Hachadgouan per rectas diuisas suas . In pascuis . in pratis . in boscho ⁊ plano cum Omnibus aisia
mentis ad illas terras pertinentibus  tenendum de me ⁊ de heredibus meis in perpetuam elemosinam pro salute anime mee ⁊ animabus predecessorum
et successorum meorum . Concedo etiam eis in elemosinam perpetuo decimam totius Redditus mei de tota terra mea . videlicet de Coriis . de blado
de caseo . de farina . de brais . de martis . de multonibus . de porcis . ⁊ de meis venationibus cum carne ⁊ Coriis . ⁊ decimam de placitis ⁊ de
Releuiis meis tam in denariis quam in ceteris adquisitionibus . Concedo etiam eis ut accipiant in nemoribus meis materiem ad edificia sua quantum
eis necesse fuerit . ⁊ ut habeant communem pasturam ad pecunias suas cum pecuniis meis ⁊ cum pecuniis hominum meorum in terris meis sine omni
disturbatione ⁊ inquietatione . Quare volo vt predicti Canonici predictas Elemosinas habeant ⁊ teneant plenarie de me ⁊ heredibus meis . ita Libere ⁊ quiete  sicut aliquam elemosinam liberius ⁊ quietius in Scotia habent ⁊ possident . Hiis Testibus . Adam Episcopo Abbirdonensi .
Omero Archidiacono . Alexandro Decano . Malcolmo . Willelmo . Edwardo Alano . Canonicis . Magistro Willelmo . Johanne de caledoure clericis . Jacobo filio Moregrundi
Duncane fratre ipsius . ⁊ multis aliis .

Malcolm, son of Morgan Earl of Mar, To all who shall view or hear these letters, Greeting: Know that I have granted, and by his my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Andrew the apostle in Scotia and the canons serving God in that place now and in the future, the church of St Moluoc at Tarland, which my father gave them, with teinds, and oblations, and all revenues pertaining to the same church, and the land which belongs to the said church, with a mill built on the same land, and a ploughgate of land which is called ‘Hachadgouan’ by its right marches; in pastures, in meadows, in wood and plain, with all easements pertaining to those lands, to be held of me and my heirs in perpetual alms, for the welfare of my soul, and the souls of my ancestors and successors. I also grant in perpetual alms the teind of my whole render from my whole land, namely, of hides, grain, cheese, flour, malt, fatted cattle, muttons, pigs, and of my game, with meat and hides, and the teind of my pleas and reliefs, in cash as well as other income. I also allow to them that they may receive material from my woods for their buildings, as much as they might need, and and that they should have common pasture for their livestock with my own livestock and the livestock of my own men on my lands without any disturbance and annoyance. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid canons should fully have and hold the aforesaid alms of me and my heirs as freely and quietly as they freely and quietly have and possess any alms in Scotia. With these witnesses : Adam, bishop of Aberdeen; Omer the archdeacon; Alexander the dean; Malcolm, William, Edward, Alan, canons; Master William, John of Calder, clerks; James son of Morgan, Duncan his brother, and many others.
